note: Internal sanity only, per invariant I4 this is NOT verification. A separate agent verifies independently.

checks.result: PASS, all tests in package including pre-existing 3.1.1/3.1.2 tests
PASS, all packages (engine, btree, catalog, graph, mvcc, split, wal)

validation_matrix_coverage: all 8 rows in validation-matrix.json covered by the above test runs
known_limitations_disclosed_not_hidden: Compact does not currently guard against concurrent AppendEdge racing a live compaction on the same node (ReadNode is not synchronized against TruncateNode via a shared lock beyond EdgeLog.mu's writer-cache protection) — issue #15's 3.1.3 test spec does not require concurrent compaction+append coverage (unlike 3.1.2's explicit -race requirement for TestPerNodeEdgeLog), so this is disclosed as an open question for verification/future subtasks (e.g. 3.1.6's round-trip test) rather than silently assumed safe.

Post-rename, pre-truncation crash window can cause a retried compaction to double-count ENTITY_COOCCUR weight for not-yet-truncated nodes — documented as an accepted risk in compact.go's package doc comment and docs/LLD/graph.md, not hidden.
